authentication: Never expose raw JWT in client-side storage. Prefer HttpOnly secure cookies.
Hash passwords using bcrypt or argon2 with strong cost factor.
Implement refresh token rotation and server-side token revocation.
Validate login inputs and enforce account lockout or rate limiting.
Store secrets only in environment variables, never in source code.
payments: Never log full card or payment instrument details.
Validate amount and currency server-side before charging.
Use idempotency keys for payment creation APIs.
Verify webhook signatures before processing events.
Keep payment provider secrets out of frontend bundles.
api: Validate request payloads with strict schema validation.
Authorize all sensitive endpoints with role or ownership checks.
Return sanitized error messages without stack traces.
Rate-limit public endpoints and login flows.
Use parameterized queries for all database operations.
ui: Avoid blocking rendering with heavy synchronous work.
Use skeleton loading or progressive rendering for slow views.
Prevent DOM XSS by sanitizing untrusted HTML content.
Ensure accessible labels, roles, and keyboard navigation.
Avoid unnecessary re-renders through memoization where needed.
performance: Avoid O(n^2) operations on hot request paths.
Use caching for expensive repeated computations.
Avoid synchronous filesystem operations in API handlers.
Batch network calls to prevent request bursts.
Use pagination for large query and response sets.
default: Generate modular, testable, and readable code.
Do not use eval, Function constructor, or unsafe dynamic execution.
Prefer least-privilege defaults and explicit validation.
Add concise comments only where intent is non-obvious.
Output in Markdown with one code block and short explanation.
